Predictive chemistry: machine learning for reaction This article is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported Licence.
deployment, reaction development, and reaction Open Access Article. Published on 28 November 2022. Downloaded on 3/28/2026 8:13:37 PM.
Cite this: Chem. Sci., 2023, 14, 226 discovery Zhengkai Tu, a Thijs Stuyver b and Connor W. Coley *ab

The eld of predictive chemistry relates to the development of models able to describe how molecules Received 12th September 2022 Accepted 25th November 2022 interact and react. It encompasses the long-standing task of computer-aided retrosynthesis, but is far more reaching and ambitious in its goals. In this review, we summarize several areas where predictive DOI: 10.1039/d2sc05089g chemistry models hold the potential to accelerate the deployment, development, and discovery of rsc.li/chemical-science organic reactions and advance synthetic chemistry.

Fig. 1 Overview of the three main categories of predictive chemistry tasks discussed throughout this review: reaction deployment, develop- ment, and discovery. It is useful to consider the extent to which each task represents an extrapolation from known reactivity to new reactivity.
